description: §5.2.3's own invalid worked example verbatim — a bare-form `onclick=` handler whose value is two semicolon-separated expressions fires E-MULTI-STATEMENT-HANDLER. W-DEAD-FUNCTION / W-PROGRAM-001 co-fire incidentally; not asserted.
rationale: §5.2.3 (L19 normative): "A bare-form event handler SHALL contain exactly one scrml expression" and "Multi-statement intent (two or more semicolon-separated expressions, or a block) SHALL force a named function. The compiler emits E-MULTI-STATEMENT-HANDLER when a bare-form handler attribute value contains a `;` outside of expression-internal contexts." The source is §5.2.3's own "Worked example — invalid (multi-statement inline)" shape: `<button onclick=startGame(); track("start")>Begin</>`. The `;` sits at top level of the opener's attribute region (not inside a string, paren, brace, bracket, template interpolation, or comment), and its owning attribute `onclick` is in the event-handler family, so the code SHALL fire.
